meta.title: About — NJ SAFETY
meta.description: Founded as Najung ETP in 1992, Najung Enterprise has focused on industrial safety wear for 34 years. From fabric R&D to despatch — every stage handled in-house.

hero.sub: NJ SAFETY has stayed within one category — flame-resistant and industrial safety wear. From fabric R&D through design, production and despatch, every stage is handled by a single one-stop system that keeps us close to the worker on the floor.
hero.asidePullEm: We engineer protection systems that preserve life

stats.desc: Time spent on a single category — flame-resistant and industrial safety wear.
Supplying power & generation, electrical works, refining & petrochemicals, energy & gas.
Fabric R&D → design → production → despatch, all managed in-house.
Chosen by industry leaders including KEPCO partners, SK and LG affiliates.

ceo.paragraph1: We started with one conviction: the safety of the people working on industrial sites can never be a matter of compromise. Thirty-four years on, that conviction has not changed.
ceo.paragraph2: Across those years we have stayed within one category — flame-resistant workwear — handling fabric, sewing, certification and after-sales in-house. The reason KEPCO partners, SK and LG affiliates and other leading Korean sites keep returning to NJ SAFETY is not that our garments look better, but that we have never once looked away from the work of protecting the people who wear them.
ceo.paragraph3: We will continue to protect the industrial floor in the closest, most certain way we know how.

phases.lede: On 5 July 1992, Najung ETP was founded to manufacture office wear, workwear, uniforms and flame-resistant garments.

items.t: Najung ETP established · in-house sewing line
Domestic production plant built · multi-line production established

phases.lede: We focused fully on flame-resistant categories and registered as a KEPCO partner, expanding nationwide.

items.t: Full expansion into FR product lines · stronger R&D
Najung ETP 30th anniversary · own website launched
FR manufacturing for KEPCO partners · nationwide supply network

phases.lede: Headquarters relocated to Sinnae-dong in Seoul as contracts with major industrial groups began in earnest.

items.t: Head office relocated and expanded (Sinnae-dong, Seoul)
Expanded contracts with SK and LG affiliates across multiple industries
Arc-flash testing completed · NFPA 2112 UL certification in progress
Brand renewal · NJ SAFETY digital platform launched

items.body: We develop high-performance aramid fabrics in-house and feed industry-specific site data back into patterns and trims, redesigning each season from the ground up.
Every fabric lot and finished garment is verified by our in-house lab and accredited third-party labs. Proof is delivered in NFPA 2112, HRC2 and Arc Flash test results.
The garment is measured against a life, not a look. Every design decision is checked against one question: does this make protection stronger?
We do not chase trends. Thirty-four years on the same ground, doing the same work, is what has earned the trust of KEPCO, SK and LG-affiliated sites.
We have not branched out. Flame-resistant industrial safety wear — every resource and every hour goes to that one category.

steps.body: Aramid blend ratios, antistatic yarn content, mesh ventilation structure — we engineer at the fabric stage.
Work-motion data gathered on site feeds directly into patterns and trim placement. Our in-house design team owns every season.
Sewing and inspection happen at our own Korean plant. With no outsourcing, quality stays consistent and feedback stays fast.
Before despatch, in-house QC confirms test reports. After despatch, A/S service carries the relationship forward.

oneStop.footQuotePre: Because every stage is run by us,
oneStop.footQuoteEm: feedback is fast and quality is consistent.

items.body: KEPCO partner · generation utilities. FR workwear for transmission, distribution and generation-plant inspection.
Dedicated protective wear for high-voltage transmission and live-line work where arc-flash risk is constant.
Turnaround and safety inspection at the Yeosu and Ulsan complexes. A line that combines antistatic protection with breathability.
High-risk protection for gas utilities, generation plants and renewable-energy infrastructure sites.

recent.body: From 2025 we have moved seriously to broaden our international certifications. Arc-flash testing has been completed, and several global certifications including NFPA 2112 UL are in progress.

rows.title: Arc-flash testing completed
rows.body: ATPV rating tests for electrical workwear completed at an accredited certification body. Official test report on file.

rows.title: NFPA 2112 UL certification in progress
rows.body: North American flash-fire protection standard — UL certification under way. Target: completion within 2026.

rows.title: Multiple international certifications in progress
rows.body: EN ISO 11612, HRC Level 2, IEC 61482 and other global standards being added.

cta.titleLine1: The FR partner closest to the floor,

capHero.sub: From fabric to despatch — how NJ Safety builds, where it serves, and the certifications that back it. Values, system, industries, and recent proof on one page.

meta.title: Contact — NJ SAFETY
meta.description: Quotes, B2B bulk orders, OEM production, certification documents and after-sales — talk to an NJ SAFETY specialist. We reply within one business day.

hero.sub: From product quotes and B2B bulk orders to OEM production — NJ SAFETY replies within one business day, clearly and directly.

items.body: General price, stock and lead-time enquiries for the Aramid season lineup.
Bulk orders from 50 units · 5% additional discount · dedicated account manager.
Custom patterns, embroidery and logo work. From fabric to sewing, produced in-house.
Requests for NFPA 2112 UL, ARC test reports and blend-ratio test data.
After-sales support for defects, size exchanges and repairs.

form.title: Inquiry form

placeholders.deliveryDate: e.g. End of July 2026 / Regular supply

form.fileHintBody: PDF · JPG · PNG · AI · ZIP / up to 20MB each
form.agreeFine: Collected: company, contact name, phone, email / Retained: 6 months after closure / Purpose: enquiry handling and quotation.
form.submitNote: — We reply within one business day
form.toastOk: ✓ Enquiry received. We will reply within one business day.

sidebar.reassurePost: — larger quotes and OEM take 2–3 days

steps.body: Enquiries submitted by form, phone or email are triaged by sales and routed to a dedicated account manager.
We confirm quantity, specification, lead time and certification requirements by phone or email. Samples sent if needed.
A formal quotation and the test-report / certification package is sent as PDF. One free revision included.
After contracting, the in-house line handles sewing, QC and despatch. Regular-supply arrangements available.

items.q: What is the minimum order quantity (MOQ)?
items.a: Bulk quotes (with a 5% additional discount) start at 50 units. OEM production (embroidery, custom patterns) starts at 100 units. Regular-supply contracts are arranged separately.

items.q: What is the average lead time?
items.a: In-stock sizes ship in 5–7 business days. New production runs take 3–6 weeks; OEM production takes 6–10 weeks. Urgent schedules can often be shortened by prior arrangement.

items.q: Can I receive test reports and certificates?
items.a: We send per-product blend-ratio, flame-resistance and ARC test reports as PDF. NFPA 2112 UL certification is targeted for completion within 2026; we can share progress documentation in the meantime.
Stock items: 100% prepayment on order. OEM and bulk quotes can be split 30% deposit / 70% on despatch. Regular-supply contracts can be settled monthly. Tax invoices issued.

items.q: How long is the after-sales period?
items.a: Manufacturing defects are exchanged or repaired free of charge within one year of despatch. Wear and tear repairs are quoted separately. Size exchanges are accepted within 14 days of despatch and only when unused.

meta.title: Size Guide — NJ SAFETY
meta.description: How to measure for industrial workwear, plus per-product size tables.

sizeGuide.intro: Safety workwear affects on-site movement directly. Use the steps below to take your measurements, then check the per-product table before ordering.

howTo.label: Chest
howTo.body: Run the tape under both armpits in a single loop, at the fullest point of the chest during a normal breath.

howTo.label: Shoulder
howTo.body: Edge to edge across the back, from one shoulder tip to the other.

howTo.label: Length
howTo.body: From the centre back neck (7th cervical vertebra) to the desired hem.

howTo.label: Sleeve
howTo.body: From the shoulder tip to the wrist, arm resting naturally at the side.

sizeGuide.tip: When you fall between two sizes, choose the larger one. Industrial workwear is often layered over base garments, so extra room is helpful.

meta.title: Privacy Policy — NJ SAFETY
meta.description: How NJ SAFETY (Najung Enterprise) collects, uses, retains, and disposes of personal information.

privacy.intro: Najung Enterprise Co., Ltd. ("the Company") complies with the Personal Information Protection Act of Korea and handles users’ personal information as follows.

sections.title: 1. Information We Collect
sections.body:
The Company collects only the minimum information needed to respond to quote inquiries and product consultations.

• Required: company name, contact name, phone, email
• Optional: attached files (drawings, specifications, evaluation documents)
• Automatically collected: access IP, cookies, service usage logs

sections.title: 2. Purpose of Collection and Use
sections.body:
• Responding to quote inquiries
• Product consultation and ordering guidance
• After-sales support
• Statistical analysis and service quality improvement

sections.title: 3. Retention and Use Period
sections.body:
• Quote and consultation inquiries: one year after the response is closed
• Active transaction records: retained per the Consumer Protection in Electronic Commerce Act and related regulations
• If consent is withdrawn or deletion is requested, data is destroyed without delay

sections.title: 4. Sharing with Third Parties
sections.body: The Company does not share users’ personal information with external parties as a rule. Exceptions are limited to lawful requests from authorities or other cases mandated by law.

sections.title: 5. Destruction Procedure and Method
sections.body:
• Procedure: destroyed immediately after the purpose is fulfilled
• Method: electronic files are permanently deleted by methods that prevent recovery; paper documents are shredded

sections.title: 6. User Rights
sections.body: Users may at any time request access, correction, deletion, or suspension of processing of their personal information. Contact the privacy officer below in writing, by phone, or by email; the Company will respond without delay.

sections.title: 7. Security Measures
sections.body:
The Company implements the following measures to keep personal information safe.

• Administrative: internal management plan, regular training
• Technical: access logs, encrypted (HTTPS) communication
• Physical: locked storage for hardcopy records

sections.title: 8. Cookies
sections.body: The Company may use cookies to improve service convenience. Users may disable cookies in their browser settings; some features may then be unavailable.

privacy.changeNote: This policy is effective as of the date above. Any future changes will be announced on this page.
